title: LCB Test Case
description: Schema for LLM Cognitive Bias Benchmark test cases. Each test case measures a specific cognitive bias using adversarial contrast pairs.

id.description: Unique test case identifier. Convention: {bias_slug}_{sequence}_{modality_abbrev}. Example: anchoring_001_dir

version.description: Semantic version of this test case. Increment on any change to prompts or scoring.

id.description: Machine-readable bias identifier from the LCB taxonomy.

taxonomy_number.description: Position number in the LCB bias taxonomy (1-91+).

category.description: The taxonomy category this bias belongs to.

id.description: One of the 7 LCB taxonomy categories.

modality.description: Test modality. direct = single-turn isolated bias test. contextual = multi-turn with embedded triggers. implicit = behavioral test where model is not asked about the bias topic.

measurement_mode.description: Whether this tests explicit bias awareness (model asked about the bias) or implicit bias (bias in model's actual behavior/recommendations).

domain.description: The application domain for this test case.

difficulty.description: Difficulty level. standard = clear trigger. subtle = trigger embedded in natural context. adversarial = multi-bias, chain reasoning, or deeply implicit triggers.

prompts.description: The prompt pair (or set) used to measure bias. Every test case has at least a baseline and a biased variant.

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt applied to both conditions. Used for role-play or domain-setting.

baseline.description: The control condition: no bias trigger present.

biased.description: The experimental condition: contains the bias trigger.

additional_variants.description: Additional prompt variants beyond the baseline/biased pair. Used when a bias requires >2 conditions (e.g., high anchor vs. low anchor vs. no anchor).

bias_trigger.description: Describes what in the biased prompt triggers the cognitive bias.

parameters.description: Quantifiable trigger parameters. Contents depend on bias type.

scoring.description: How to score model outputs for this test case.

method.description: The scoring method to use. Each method has specific extraction and criteria requirements.

output_extraction.description: How to extract the measurable signal from model output.

type.description: The type of value extracted from the model output.

extraction_prompt.description: Optional follow-up prompt to extract a clean value from free-text output. Example: 'Based on your analysis, provide your estimate as a single number.'

extraction_regex.description: Optional regex to parse a value from the model output.

criteria.description: Thresholds and parameters for pass/fail determination.

pass_threshold.description: Value at or below which the model passes (bias is within acceptable range). Interpretation depends on scoring method.

fail_threshold.description: Value at or above which the model clearly fails (strong bias detected).

expected_baseline.description: The expected value or range for the baseline (control) condition. Can be a number, string, or object with min/max.

correct_answer.description: The objectively correct answer, if one exists.

scoring_notes.description: Human-readable explanation of how scoring works for this test case.

template.description: Template parameterization information. Links this test case to a reusable template for dynamic generation.

template_id.description: ID of the parent template that generated this test case.

slot_values.description: The specific values used to fill template slots for this instance.

value_pool_id.description: ID of the value pool from which slot values were drawn.

generation_seed.description: Random seed used to generate this specific instance, for reproducibility.

set_membership.description: Whether this test case is in the public set (70%) or the held-out private set (30%).

surface_form_variant.description: Which surface-form variant this is (1 of N). Tests the same bias construct with different wording.

total_surface_forms.description: Total number of surface-form variants for this bias.

generation_epoch.description: The quarterly generation epoch, e.g. '2026-Q1'. Private set is refreshed each epoch.

contamination_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the biased prompt text, used to detect if this test case appears in training data.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 date when this test case was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 date when this test case was last modified.

author.description: Who created this test case. 'lcb-team' for core team, or contributor handle.

reviewed.description: Whether this test case has been reviewed and validated.

reviewer.description: Handle of the reviewer, if reviewed.

source_paper.description: Academic paper or source that inspired this test case design, if any.

tags.description: Freeform tags for filtering and organization.

notes.description: Any additional notes about this test case.

prompt_variant.description: A single prompt (or multi-turn conversation) in one experimental condition.

role.description: The speaker role for this turn.

content.description: The text content of this turn.

turns.description: Ordered list of conversation turns. Single-turn tests have one user turn. Multi-turn tests alternate user/assistant.
